sections.title: Aerial Lift Pre Operation Inspection and JHA
sections.html:
Task Description: ________________________________________________________________________________

Pre-Task Hazard Identification:

☐ Overhead power lines / electrical hazards

☐ Overhead obstructions / pinch points

☐ Uneven or unstable ground conditions

☐ Inadequate lighting

☐ High winds or adverse weather

☐ Hazardous atmosphere

☐ Drop-offs, holes, or trenches

☐ Falling objects

☐ Pedestrian or vehicular traffic

☐ Equipment malfunction

Required PPE:

☐ Hard Hat

☐ Full Body Harness

☐ Safety Glasses

☐ Shock-Absorbing Lanyard

☐ High-Visibility Vest

☐ Work Gloves

☐ Steel-Toe Boots

☐ Hearing Protection

sections.title: Aerial Lift Pre Operation Inspection and JHA (continued 2)
sections.html:
Job Hazard Analysis (JHA):

Step #

Task Step Description

Potential Hazard

Hazard Control Measure

Responsible Party

1

Pre-operation inspection

Missed defects, equipment failure

Complete inspection checklist thoroughly before use

Operator

2

Moving lift to work area

Struck-by, tip-over, crushing

Use spotter, check route for hazards, maintain safe speed

Operator / Spotter

3

Elevating platform

Fall, struck-by overhead objects, electrocution

Maintain 10ft clearance from power lines, wear harness/lanyard tied off to anchor point

Operator

4

Performing work from platform

Dropped objects, falls

Keep tools tethered, do not stand on guardrails, keep feet on floor

Operator

5

Lowering and parking lift

Crushing, unauthorized use

Check below before lowering, park on level ground, remove key

Operator

6

7

8

9

10

Emergency Contacts:
Nearest Hospital: ____________________________________
Emergency Number: 911 or ___________________________
Site First Aid Location: _____________________________

Aerial Lift Pre-Operation Inspection Checklist

Inspection Frequency: Prior to each work shift (per 29 CFR 1926.453(b)(2)(i) and WAC 296-880).
